title: thymus ciliatus subsp. algeriensis
intro: Thymus ciliatus subsp. algeriensis, also known as Algerian thyme, is a subspecies of thyme native to North Africa. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues and wounds. The health benefits of Algerian thyme include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, Algerian thyme is often used in teas, infusions, and essential oil forms, which can be used to soothe coughs, calm digestive issues, and promote relaxation.
